reasoning:
The stock jumped ~16% to 58.88 following a substantial Q1 2025 earnings beat on April 24, where HAS strongly beat revenue and EPS consensus. Analyst target prices were raised aggressively (to $72-85) compared to the current 58.88 level. Historically, a massive positive earnings surprise is followed by Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD), where the stock continues upward for 1-3 months. Furthermore, the pre-earnings dip down to ~49 was driven by fears of China tariffs, which management's Q1 commentary partially allayed. The close of 58.88 represents a return to its 12-month mean (55-67 range), suggesting the stock isn't overly stretched on longer timeframes. 

Expectations for positive drift give a solid >50% probability in the short-to-medium term. As the horizon stretches to July and October, the potential realization of $300M in future tariff hits (management mentioned this as a worst case scenario later in the year) and general market unpredictability draw estimates closer to the 0.52 base rate. The progression (58% -> 56% -> 54% -> 52%) reflects the high likelihood of continued short-term PEAD tapering into macro/overall market uncertainty in the longer horizons.
